title: 10 possible side effects of Cydonia oblonga

side_effects_list.side_effect_desc: Cydonia oblonga causes allergic reactions due to its high content of polyphenolic compounds and flavonoids. These bioactive molecules can trigger an immune response in some individuals, leading to symptoms such as skin rash, itching, and hives. Additionally, the fruit's seed oil may also contribute to allergic reactions, causing respiratory issues like asthma or bronchial constriction in susceptible persons.

Cydonia oblonga induces nausea violently due to its high concentration of compounds such as amylase and pectin, which can cause stomach irritation and discomfort. The consumption of large amounts or the lack of adequate digestive enzymes can lead to an overproduction of these compounds, resulting in violent nausea. Additionally, quince's bitter taste and astringent properties may also contribute to its emetic effect, making it unpleasant for some individuals to consume.

Cydonia oblonga produces skin rashes in some individuals due to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenes. These compounds can cause an allergic reaction or irritation on the skin, leading to redness, itching, and inflammation. The rashes may appear as hives, blisters, or patches on the face, arms, or legs. Additionally, the fruit's acidic properties can also contribute to skin irritation, making it essential for users to be aware of these potential reactions when handling or consuming Cydonia oblonga.

Cydonia oblonga causes stomach upset in some individuals due to its high tannin content and potential allergic reactions. The tannins can irritate the mucous membranes of the digestive tract, leading to discomfort, nausea, and abdominal pain. Additionally, people who are sensitive to the plant's allergenic compounds may experience adverse reactions, which can manifest as stomach upset, diarrhea, or vomiting.

Cydonia oblonga affects blood pressure by interacting with the body's vascular system. The quercetin and chlorogenic acids present in quince can cause a reduction in blood pressure due to their vasodilatory effects, which lead to increased blood flow and a decrease in peripheral resistance. Additionally, the potassium content in quince may contribute to this effect by counteracting the actions of sodium and promoting vasodilation.

Cydonia oblonga increases heart rate due to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can stimulate the cardiovascular system. These bioactive compounds enhance the sympathetic nervous system's activity, leading to increased heart rate and blood pressure. Additionally, quince's ability to modulate calcium channels and potassium currents in cardiac cells may also contribute to this effect, resulting in a heightened cardiovascular response.

Cydonia oblonga triggers anxiety attacks in some individuals due to its high content of anthraquinone glycosides. These compounds can stimulate the nervous system, leading to increased heart rate and blood pressure, which can exacerbate feelings of anxiety and panic. The fruit's bitter taste and aroma may also contribute to anxiogenic effects, as some people may experience discomfort or unease when consuming strong flavors or odors.

Cydonia oblonga leads to insomnia problems due to its high content of amygdalin and terpenoids. These compounds can stimulate the nervous system, leading to restlessness, anxiety, and difficulty falling asleep. Additionally, the quercetin present in Cydonia oblonga can cause a temporary increase in cortisol levels, further disrupting sleep patterns. As a result, some individuals may experience insomnia when using Cydonia oblonga.

Cydonia oblonga causes muscle cramps severely due to its high concentration of flavonoids and saponins. These compounds can alter the body's electrolyte balance, leading to changes in muscle function and contractions that can result in painful muscle cramps. Additionally, quince's potential interaction with certain medications, such as diuretics, may also contribute to this side effect by further disrupting electrolyte levels and exacerbating muscle spasms.

Cydonia oblonga induces headache episodes frequently due to its high concentration of coumarin and other bioactive compounds. These compounds can cause blood vessels to constrict, leading to a drop in blood flow to the brain, which may trigger headaches. Additionally, the flavonoids present in Cydonia oblonga may also play a role in inducing headache episodes by affecting the body's natural pain-relieving mechanisms. As a result, individuals using Cydonia oblonga may experience recurring headache episodes.

intro: Cydonia oblonga has some side effects when used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and skin irritation. The causes of these side effects are often attributed to the herb's high concentration of coumarins, which can cause digestive problems in large doses or interact with certain medications. In severe cases, these side effects can worsen daily life by causing persistent abdominal pain, skin rashes, or even anaphylaxis, leading to missed workdays, social withdrawal, and decreased quality of life.
intro_desc: Cydonia oblonga has some side effects when used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues and skin irritation. These problems can be caused by consuming excessive amounts or using the herb in a manner not suitable for its intended use. In severe cases, gastrointestinal issues can lead to malnutrition and dehydration, while skin irritation can result in painful blisters and scarring, potentially affecting one's social and professional life.
